name: High-Protein Chicken Stir-Fry
description: Quick and easy 30-minute dinner packed with 45g protein per serving. Restaurant quality at home.

steps.title: Prep the chicken
steps.instruction: Cut chicken breast into 1-inch cubes. Pat completely dry with paper towels to ensure maximum browning. Season generously with salt, pepper, and a pinch of cornstarch. Toss to coat.
steps.proTip: Dry chicken = better browning! The cornstarch creates a light crispy coating.

steps.title: Mix the sauce
steps.instruction: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, and 1 tsp cornstarch dissolved in 2 tbsp water. Set aside.

steps.title: Heat the wok
steps.instruction: Heat wok or large non-stick pan over high heat for 2 minutes until smoking hot. Add sesame oil and swirl to coat.
steps.proTip: A screaming hot wok is the #1 secret to restaurant-quality stir-fry.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.instruction: Add chicken in a SINGLE LAYER — do not overcrowd. Cook undisturbed for 2 minutes until golden. Toss and cook 2 more minutes until cooked through. Remove from pan and set aside.
steps.proTip: Resist the urge to stir! Let it sear for a crust.

steps.title: Stir-fry the vegetables
steps.instruction: Add broccoli and bell pepper to the hot wok. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp and slightly charred. Return chicken to the pan.
steps.proTip: You want a slight char on the veggies — that's flavor!

steps.title: Add sauce & serve
steps.instruction: Pour sauce over everything. Toss vigorously for 30 seconds until sauce coats everything and thickens. Serve immediately over brown rice. Garnish with spring onions.
steps.proTip: Move fast — the sauce thickens quickly!

name: Overnight Protein Oats
description: Prep once, eat breakfast all week. 32g protein with zero morning cooking required.

steps.title: Mix the dry ingredients
steps.instruction: Add oats, protein powder, and chia seeds to a mason jar or airtight container. Mix until evenly combined.
steps.proTip: A wide-mouth mason jar is perfect — you can eat straight from it!

steps.title: Add wet ingredients
steps.instruction: Add Greek yogurt and almond milk. Stir everything vigorously until the protein powder is fully dissolved and no clumps remain.
steps.proTip: Slightly warm milk dissolves protein powder much more easily.

steps.title: Refrigerate overnight
steps.instruction: Cover tightly and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, ideally overnight (8 hours). The oats will absorb all liquid and turn thick and creamy.
steps.proTip: Prep 5 jars on Sunday for a whole week of breakfasts!

steps.title: Top and serve
steps.instruction: In the morning, give it a stir. Top with sliced banana, a drizzle of honey, or fresh berries. Eat cold or microwave for 60 seconds.

name: Salmon & Sweet Potato Sheet Pan
description: One pan, minimal cleanup — omega-3 rich salmon with perfectly roasted sweet potato.

steps.title: Preheat & prep pan
steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F). Line a large sheet pan with parchment paper.
steps.proTip: Overcrowding = steaming, not roasting. Use two pans if needed!

steps.title: Season & roast sweet potato
steps.instruction: Toss sweet potato cubes with 1 tbsp olive oil, paprika, salt and pepper. Spread in a single layer. Roast 15 minutes.
steps.proTip: Cut potatoes evenly so they cook at the same rate.

steps.title: Add green beans
steps.instruction: Push sweet potato to one side. Add green beans tossed in olive oil, salt and pepper. Roast another 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add salmon & finish
steps.instruction: Place salmon fillets skin-side down. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon squeeze. Roast 10-12 minutes until salmon flakes easily.
steps.proTip: Salmon is done when it turns opaque and flakes — don't overcook!

steps.title: Rest & serve
steps.instruction: Remove from oven. Squeeze remaining lemon over everything. Let salmon rest 2 minutes. Plate with vegetables.
steps.proTip: Resting allows juices to redistribute — worth the 2 minutes!
